title: Podman >= 5.2 changed the meaning of --pull (without argument)
body:
### Issue Description

Hi,

It seems podman >= 5.2 did change the meaning of `podman build --pull` (without argument for `--pull`). It used to behave as `--pull=always` (like Docker does), and now it behaves as `--pull=missing`.

This issue was actually raised already in #5723, where @flouthoc already replied that it's an intentional change, but the pull request referenced in this comment https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/5723#issuecomment-2695641234 is actually looping to the very same issue #5723. So in the end, I don't know the rational behind this ;) Knowing how important it is for the podman project to stick to docker behavior, I am really interested to understand why it was chosen on purpose to differ from docker here (and I can read in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/20124 that before that, at least in 2020 and 2023, it was important for you that `--pull` actually meant `--pull=always`).

I am asking because internally in Amadeus this created some small incident today. We still use a mix of docker & podman, expecting both to work the same way, and... we discovered the hard way that `--pull` doesn't do what we expect anymore when using podman... (today, for an internal reason, some old internal images we had stopped working, and some of our developers were still using these old versions as --pull no longer automatically fetches the new ones) This change of behavior doesn't seem to be documented in the podman 5.2 release notes.

In the release notes for buildah 1.37.0, 3 changes mentions `--pull`: #5583, #5605 and #5407 However I don't see discussed in these pull requests or the associated issues the docker compatibility break. I "just" see documentation related motivations.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

.

### Describe the results you received

`podman build --pull` differs from `docker build --pull`.

### Describe the results you expected

`podman build --pull` used to behave like docker, and somehow I had hoped it continues to work the same way.

### buildah version output

```yaml
.
```

### buildah info output

```yaml
.
```

### Provide your storage.conf

```toml
.
```

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: I think this was actually in #5406 and #5407, combined with #5605, where the rationale was to bring it in line with `podman run`, though I think at the time I was mistaken about the then-current behavior.
So there is still a possibility that the historical docker behavior is brought back by default ?

IMO we should restore the original behavior. We should avoid causing regressions as much as possible and given this is clearly one and also now differs from docker I think it makes sense to fix this. missing is already the default without `--pull` so in that sense --pull was made useless.

cc @flouthoc
